---
title: "Research Projects"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    css: styles.css
---

<p style="color:#6b7280; font-size:1.05rem; margin-bottom:30px;">
Below are selected graduate research projects from my time at Emory University. 
Each project applies advanced statistical methods to real-world public health questions.
</p>

---

```{=html}
<div class="project-card">
  <h3>Bayesian Spatial Modeling of Opioid-Related Mortality in Georgia</h3>
  <div class="project-meta">
    <i class="fa fa-calendar"></i> May 2025 &ndash; Present &nbsp;&nbsp;
    <i class="fa fa-user"></i> Advisor: Professor Lance Waller, Emory University
  </div>
  <span class="tag">Bayesian Statistics</span>
  <span class="tag">Spatial Analysis</span>
  <span class="tag">R</span>
  <span class="tag">Public Health</span>
  <p>
    The opioid crisis has had a devastating and uneven impact across communities in Georgia.
    This project maps opioid mortality disparities at the county level using Bayesian spatial models,
    identifying persistent high-risk geographic clusters that can inform targeted public health interventions.
  </p>
  <p>
    We integrate sociodemographic factors — including poverty rate and racial composition — into
    multivariable spatial models. Model fit is compared using the Deviance Information Criterion (DIC),
    and results are reported as posterior mortality rates with 95% credible intervals. A simulation
    study evaluates how neighbor-weight matrices and spatial correlation strength affect precision and
    statistical power to detect true geographic disparities.
  </p>
  <p>
    <strong>Key methods:</strong> Besag-York-Mollié (BYM) model, Intrinsic Conditional Autoregressive
    (ICAR) priors, Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) inference, choropleth mapping.
  </p>
  <a class="btn-project" href="https://github.com/feiran924" target="_blank">
    <i class="fa fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
  </a>
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="project-card">
  <h3>Multi-Pollutant Modeling of Air Pollution Effects on Gestational Complications</h3>
  <div class="project-meta">
    <i class="fa fa-calendar"></i> October 2024 &ndash; Present &nbsp;&nbsp;
    <i class="fa fa-user"></i> Advisor: Professor Howard H. Chang, Emory University
  </div>
  <span class="tag">Environmental Epidemiology</span>
  <span class="tag">Survival Analysis</span>
  <span class="tag">R</span>
  <span class="tag">Exposure Assessment</span>
  <p>
    Air pollution exposure during pregnancy is a significant concern for maternal and fetal health.
    This project investigates the joint and individual effects of 12 ambient air pollutants on
    gestational complications using a large Georgia birth cohort (conceptions 2007–2013).
  </p>
  <p>
    We employ logistic regression and discrete-time survival models to estimate trimester-specific
    risk, accounting for high-dimensional pollutant exposures alongside demographic and socioeconomic
    confounders. Spatiotemporal pollution estimates are linked to birth records to build a
    comprehensive, analysis-ready cohort. Results highlight critical exposure windows and key
    pollutant-outcome pairs, providing actionable guidance for environmental health policy.
  </p>
  <p>
    <strong>Key methods:</strong> Weighted Quantile Sum (WQS) regression, Bayesian Kernel Machine
    Regression (BKMR), discrete-time hazard models, spatiotemporal data integration.
  </p>
  <a class="btn-project" href="https://github.com/feiran924" target="_blank">
    <i class="fa fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
  </a>
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="footer-section">
  &copy; 2025 Feiran Zhang &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; Built with R Markdown &amp; GitHub Pages
</div>
```
